Sense.Definition: in the back of, in the rear, behind
to marry, to marry and live at
Includes both the wart-hog and the wild pig
Long hair, with bushy, unborn head: see
Circles on a traditional leather cushion
Procession of horsemen in line and swarm of bees
The second present a wooer to his prospective parents in law
at the end of a sword hilt
Hyaena which can become a human
Oribi; small African antelope, red flanked
The holes of the seed, the plants therein and fig.
Spear like missile with a rope attached
Bird with a strong hooked and toothed bill
Red false hem to a gown
Half-brothers(or sisters) having the same father
are you spending the evening well?
Fragment of substance, as of crumbled cooked food
Large food and game fish of the family latidae bappoore
Excretory opening at the end of the alimentary canal
Attached to the nostrils of an ox
Tradition of prophet of the prophet Muhammad (SAWS) SEE HADIISEWOL
The largest kind of gown: see
hyaena which can become a human
edible seeds of various leguminous plants
A kind of gown with short sleeves see jaba2
Alfromosia SP.a tree pale leave and flat pods
the joy prolonged high _pitched sounds of women
I do not see see what I am to get
have you spent the night well?
Fragment of substance,as of crumbled cooked food
Fences made of wooden stakes with cornstalk tied to it forming a solid fence
cows kept for milking, see diilaaye
Trees with edible yellow 'plums'and the fruits
At, to, until, from, UpTo, haha saare, at
Fleshy hind part of the human legs below the knees
Fleshy hind parts of the human legs below the knees
Male organ of copulation and (in mammals) urination
The flat part of the two sides of the head between the forehead and the ear
I have come to visit you
have you spent the morning well?
to end, to bring to an end
Coloured mats imported from the Cameroons
Egg also testes, especially of boys when they first drop
A skin disorder in which patches of skin tend to lose its natural colour
places where things are kept see resrude
superlative good or bad for. capable
one's with gaps in the teeth
Large pot always kept in the hut: see
Cows of the colours of bush fowl
Whole calabash with a small hand_hole in the top
Two or more men married to sisters
grass-roofed huts through which entrances are made to a compound;
barbs to a spear or arrow;
barbs to a spear or arrow;
to set up a stick or pole
A coloured mat imported from the Cameroons
A child whose mother dies before it is weaned
one's who have lost the full use of his limb
Water marsh of resembling coarse grass
Thorny shrub with edible red brown berries
The green leaves of the tobacco plant
Edible seeds of various leguminous plants
At the end of a sword_hilt
I have to come on a visit
How have we spent the day (pl)?
in front of, in front, at the front
to greet someone with salaamu aleykum
Fronds of the young doum plamvbalol
Inner suspended bands of a hut
right to use and profit from somebody's property
Waterside or marsh plant resembling coarse
Mats made of shoots from the palm
Lines coursed by folding or crushing
whole calabash with a small hand hole in the top
The knob at the end of the handle of a knife
But in the case of a dagger to lend weight
The fruit of the jujube tree
Cows with horns drooping unfastened towards the ears
Woman's fibre covers for a load
The long straight shoots of the combretum species shrub
Both the sweet and the salanum potato
the relationship between the child of an aunt and that of an uncle
Lines coursed by folding or crushing
The green leaves of the tobacco plant
Cows with white body and black face
The outer edge of a cloth or mat
The brown bus hornet; see njabattu.
Cows similar in colour to gerlaaye
To be mentally deficient person incapable of rational canduct
How have we spent the evening?
See fronds of the young doum palm
the four books; tawreeta, jabuura, linjiila, alkur'aana, the books given to the prophets; the pentatech, the psalms, the gospel, the koran
The holes in the game tile
Stiffening of the neck in cattle
Cows with white body and black face
The piece of calabash for dishing porridge up
the region between the tropics of cancer and Capricorn
Anyone of two or more men married to sisters
he accepted with the right hand
Calabash for turning milk into sour
Women who were marr, whether widow,or divorcee
